mnemonics: Put Clocks in the Right Order
keyPoints: Logical time is a concept used in distributed systems to synchronize clocks across different nodes.
Logical clocks are used to prioritize events and ensure that operations are executed in the correct order.
Logical time is not necessarily tied to physical time, allowing for flexibility in distributed systems.
Logical clocks often use a monotonic clock, which increases over time but does not reset.
Distributed systems can use logical clocks to resolve conflicts and ensure consistency among nodes.
